title: Quiz: Quantum Support Vector Machines
description: Test your understanding of QSVM concepts

questions.question: In a Quantum SVM, which part runs on the quantum computer?
questions.explanation: In QSVM, only the quantum kernel K(x,y) = |⟨φ(x)|φ(y)⟩|² is computed on the quantum computer. The SVM optimization (finding support vectors and dual variables) is performed classically.

questions.question: What is the quantum kernel formula in QSVM?
questions.explanation: The quantum kernel is K(x,y) = |⟨0|U†(x)U(y)|0⟩|², which measures the overlap between quantum states prepared by the feature map U(x) and U(y).

questions.question: What is a key advantage of using quantum kernels in SVM?

options.text: Access to feature spaces difficult to compute classically

questions.explanation: Quantum kernels can compute inner products in feature spaces that may be intractable to compute classically, potentially capturing complex patterns in the data.

questions.question: The regularization parameter C in QSVM controls the trade-off between margin width and training error.
questions.explanation: True. Just like in classical SVM, the C parameter in QSVM controls how much we penalize training errors vs. having a wider margin. Higher C means stricter margin with more focus on minimizing errors.

questions.question: For a K-class problem using One-vs-One strategy, how many binary classifiers are needed?
questions.explanation: One-vs-One requires training a classifier for each pair of classes. With K classes, there are K(K-1)/2 unique pairs, so K(K-1)/2 classifiers are needed.

questions.question: Which strategy is typically more efficient for many-class problems?
questions.explanation: One-vs-Rest (OvR) requires only K classifiers (one per class), while One-vs-One requires K(K-1)/2. For large K, OvR is significantly more efficient in terms of training and quantum kernel evaluations.

questions.question: What hyperparameter in the quantum kernel controls the 'width' or sensitivity?
questions.explanation: The gamma parameter (or similar scaling parameter) in the quantum kernel controls how quickly the kernel value decays with distance between points, affecting the model's sensitivity to local patterns.

questions.question: Which of the following affect QSVM performance? (Select all)
questions.explanation: All of these affect QSVM performance: the feature map determines what patterns can be captured, C controls regularization, repetitions affect expressibility, and hardware noise degrades kernel accuracy.
